main.name: Ayyah Saleh
main.bio: Henry Foster Scholarship Award recipient at UNL Law, dedicated to leveraging diverse legal experience in corporate litigation, immigration, and probate law. Skilled in legal research, client advocacy, and case management. Multilingual professional fluent in Arabic, Spanish, Dari, and Farsi. Seeking opportunities to apply my varied background and language abilities while pursuing my J.D. Committed to excellence in legal studies and practice.

education.description: President of Multicultural Legal Society, Treasurer of American Constitution Society, Library Advisory Board

education.degree: Bachelor of Science - Criminal Justice and Corrections

work.title: Summer Law Clerk
work.description: Performing document review for litigation defense matters. Analyzing and researching issues relating to tort defense and bodily injury. Examining case strategy to determine best client outcomes

work.title: Paralegal and Billing Clerk
work.description: Managed account and billing services for a law firm with over 600 clients, including monthly client invoicing and IOLTA billing. Provided professional translation services for clients. (Arabic, Spanish, Dari, and Farsi). Prepared multiple adjustments of status applications: met with clients, gathered and organized sensitive material, researched evidence supporting their claims, and compiled their petitions per USCIS guidelines. Wrote Multiple Freedom of Information Act Requests for clients in immigration detention.

work.title: Grant Manager
work.description: Drafted and submitted federal and state grant applications. Project Management for immigrant advocacy networks. Founded Interstate Immigrant Outreach Initiatives

projects.title: IP and Investment Policy Research
projects.description: At UNL Law, I'm collaborating with Dr. Christal Sheppard on cutting-edge research. We're exploring innovative intellectual property and securities policies to boost Iraq's economic development. This project blends my interests in IP law, emerging markets, and international monetary policy, preparing me for future challenges in global tech law.
projects.category: I'm working with Dr. Christal Sheppard to develop policy solutions to boost economic development in Iraq

projects.title: Cryptocurrency Regulation Research
projects.description: Submitting research on cryptocurrency regulation in fintech to Nebraska Law Review Online. Exploring the balance between innovation and consumer protection in digital currencies, addressing key legal and policy considerations for the future.
projects.category: Submitting research on cryptocurrency regulation to the Nebraska Law Review

projects.title: Moot Court
projects.description: This semester, I'm honing my advocacy skills by competing in UNL Law's moot court program. This challenging experience will sharpen my legal reasoning, enhance my oral arguments, and deepen my understanding of complex legal issues. It's an exciting opportunity to apply classroom knowledge to simulated courtroom scenarios.
projects.category: I'm competing in moot court to sharpen my legal reasoning this fall.
